key_factors: Argentina is ranked 1st in the official FIFA rankings, while Switzerland is ranked 14th.
Argentina reached the quarter-finals after a 3-2 victory over Egypt, while Switzerland advanced past Colombia on penalties after a scoreless 120 minutes.
Switzerland forward Johan Manzambi is officially ruled out with a knee injury, creating a confirmed key starter absence asymmetry in favor of Argentina.

Argentina traveled approximately 700 miles from Atlanta to Kansas City, while Switzerland traveled approximately 1,600 miles from Vancouver. This 900-mile travel difference exceeds the 800-mile threshold and warrants a 2-point adjustment in favor of Argentina.
Very warm and humid conditions (around 85-90°F at kickoff) are forecast in Kansas City, affecting both teams equally (no weather asymmetry).
The referee is Portuguese official João Pinheiro, who has issued an average of 2.5 yellow cards and 0.5 red cards per match in his 2 refereed games this tournament.
unverified_or_unknown: The exact starting lineups and tactical approaches are not officially confirmed at prediction time.
The exact yellow/red card averages for both squads are partially unconfirmed, as complete tournament-wide stats are aggregated from secondary sources.
